---
title: "5.1.3 HTTP and HTTPS"
subtitle: "The internet and the World Wide Web · P181-P182"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.76em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## HTTP

### Transfer rules

<span class="term">Hypertext transfer protocol (http)</span> (超文本传输协议) is a set of rules that must be obeyed when transferring files across the internet.

The protocol controls how web page data is transferred between devices and web servers.

## HTTPS

### Secure transfer

When some form of security, for example <span class="term">SSL</span> or <span class="term">TLS</span>, is used, http changes to <span class="term">https</span>.

The <span class="mark">'s' stands for secure</span>, and indicates a more secure way of sending and receiving data across a network.

## Secure Clues

### What users see

When HTTPS is used, users may see:

::: {.tight-list}
- `https` at the start of the URL
- the green padlock symbol in the status bar or address bar
- a secure connection when data is sent across a network
:::

The textbook later connects this to <span class="term">Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)</span>.

## Classroom Check

### HTTP vs HTTPS

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- http is a set of rules for transferring files across the internet
- https is used when security such as SSL or TLS is applied
- the `s` in https stands for secure
- https indicates a <span class="mark">more secure way of sending and receiving data</span>
- the padlock symbol can indicate a secure connection
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Web browsers

The next deck explains the browser features that help users access and use web pages.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
